title: HOB-147
content:
{{Top|fga|leg}} {{Character |type=Galactic Republic |image=[[File:HOB-147_CWA9.jpg]] |name=HOB-147 |homeworld=[[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]]{{DB|organization|clonetroopers|clone troopers}} |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] {{C|[[Cloning/Legends|clone]]}}{{CWACite|9|Salvaged}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |donor=[[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] |height=1.83 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Black]] |feathers= |eyes=Brown |skin=Tan |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] **[[Light Brigade Division]] *[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|I knew you were tough.|Nia, whispering to HOB-147 after he saved her and her friends' lives|}} '''HOB-147''' was a [[Clone trooper pilot/Legends|clone pilot]] who flew a [[V-19 Torrent starfighter/Legends|V-19 Torrent starfighter]] for [[Light Brigade Division]] in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]]. He was shot down in a [[Starfighter combat/Legends|space battle]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], but survived in his [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]], and floated through space, unconscious. Shortly after [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]]—one of the [[Contingency Orders for the Grand Army of the Republic: Order Initiation, Orders 1 Through 150|GAR's 150 contingency orders]] that instructed the clone forces to eliminate all [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]—was issued, he was rescued by the junk [[Scavenger/Legends|salvager]] [[Hurd Coyle]]. Order 66 began the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]], the near-eradication of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], which allowed the then [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] to reorganize the Republic into the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and declare himself [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]]. After being rescued, 147 first assumed that the salvager was working for the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatists]] when he saw re-programmed [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] working throughout the ship, and found a group of Jedi [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|younglings]] in the [[Starship/Legends|ship's]] cargo bay. Believing that Coyle had kidnapped them, 147 was surprised to discover that Hurd Coyle was in fact transporting the younglings to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] in order to save them from the new Galactic Empire. 147 then decided to disobey Order 66, and did not inform his fellow [[Cloning/Legends|clones]] that Coyle was transporting the children. ==Biography== ===Rescue=== {{Quote|Sir, there's a V-19 ''Torrent starfighter'' in view. Damage seems extensive.''"
''"Pull it aboard and see if we can salvage anything from it. Just 'cause we're on a mission don't mean we can't still make a few bucks!|A battle droid and Coyle discuss what to do with HOB-147's damaged ship|}} The [[Clone trooper pilot/Legends|clone trooper pilot]] HOB-147 was one of the many [[Cloning/Legends|clones]] of the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] created on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]] to serve in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]]. Shortly before the [[Battle of Utapau/Legends|Battle of Utapau]], HOB-147 flew a [[V-19 Torrent starfighter/Legends|V-19 Torrent starfighter]] for [[Light Brigade Division]] in a [[Starfighter combat/Legends|space battle]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. During the battle, 147's [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] sustained heavy damage and he was sent adrift in [[Realspace/Legends|space]]. Although the V-19 was badly damaged, the [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]] was not penetrated, and the [[Life support/Legends|life support]] was still active, keeping him alive. [[File:HOB-147_ship_CWA9.jpg|thumb|left|250px|HOB-147's damaged V-19 Torrent starfighter adrift in space.]] 147 drifted for several weeks, unconscious, and during this time, he missed the Battle of Utapau, the last major battle of the Clone Wars. Additionally, he missed [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] declaration of [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]]—one of the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic's]] [[Contingency Orders for the Grand Army of the Republic: Order Initiation, Orders 1 Through 150|150 contingency measures]] that ordered the clone forces to eliminate any Jedi in the area, as they were considered a threat to the Republic. This measure allowed Palpatine to declare himself [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], and create the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] out of the Old Republic. After a while, HOB's starfighter drifted near a [[Hurd Coyle's ship|ship]] owned by [[Hurd Coyle]], a [[Scavenger/Legends|salvager]] passing through the area. A reprogrammed [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]] that was working for Coyle detected the clone's ship and informed his master. Coyle then ordered the starfighter to be brought aboard, thinking that even though he was on a mission, he could still make a few [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] from salvaging it. When a group of B1s went down to the [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] to dismantle the ship, and see if anything could be salvaged, they discovered that 147 was still alive. When they told Coyle, he reluctantly ordered one of his [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] to bring 147 to the ship's sick bay. ===Discovering Coyle's mission=== {{Quote|Kidnapping children, Order 66? What kind of Separatist plot is this?|HOB-147, assuming that Coyle was working for the Separatists|}} When 147 awoke, he discovered that he was surrounded by [[Droid/Legends|droids]]. Thinking that he had been captured by the Separatists, the clone jumped out of his bed, and started to destroy the droids with his bare hands. When Coyle walked in, he was surprised to see that 147 had wrecked his new droids. In an attempt to stop the clone, the [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] shot 147 with an [[electro-dart]], knocking him down and completely paralyzing him. While 147 was incapacitated, Coyle dragged the clone to a small room and chained his leg to the wall. Regaining consciousness, 147 found himself chained to the wall of the room, with Coyle sitting to his side. Coyle told the pilot about Order 66, and how the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] was now considered an enemy by Palpatine. 147 was surprised at this, and didn't completely believe it. When Coyle walked away, the clone sat down and started to think things through. While he was thinking, a female Jedi youngling, [[Nia]], appeared in the vent system on the wall, and began to watch 147. He saw her there, and quickly ripped the cover off the vent and pulled Nia out. While 147 asked the girl who she was, Coyle walked into the room, and seeing what was happening, shot 147 with another electro-dart, and dragged Nia out of the room. [[File:Electro-dart_Clone.jpg|thumb|right|250px|HOB-147 is hit by an electro-dart]] Once 147 had once again recovered and gotten back to his feet, he now felt certain that whatever was going on was a Separatist plot, and that he needed to escape. The clone ripped the metal chain off the wall, and ran through the salvager's ship, looking for a way out. Eventually, he paused at a corner and found a [[B2-series super battle droid/Legends|B2 super battle droid]] guarding the door to the hold. HOB destroyed the battle droid and decided to look inside the hold, but as he walked into the room, he was attacked by an unknown, invisible force, and passed out. When he regained consciousness, 147 discovered that there were Jedi younglings all around him, and that the unseen force that attacked him was likely [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. 147 kicked the younglings away from himself, and discovered that Coyle had at least eleven younglings aboard his ship, including Nia and [[Bon (Jedi)|Bon]]; it turned out that Coyle had taken on a mission to transport the Jedi younglings to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]. Nia went up to 147, thinking that he wouldn't hurt her. She asked him whether all clones were bad or not. Touched by her childlike innocence, HOB decided to disobey Order 66 and his programming, and help the Jedi escape from the new Empire. Meanwhile, Coyle's ship was boarded by a group of [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] patrolling the area, suspecting he was hiding something aboard. When Coyle went to greet them, their [[Unidentified ARC trooper commander|ARC trooper commander]] threatened to order his men to tear the ship apart in order to find anything incriminating on board, although Coyle begged them not to. HOB-147 then walked in, saying that Coyle had saved his life, and that he had been up and down the ship and had found nothing wrong. The ARC trooper believed the pilot's lie, and ordered his men back to their [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]]. As 147 walked out, he saw Nia waving to him from a nearby vent, saying she knew he was tough. ==Personality and traits== HOB-147, who was trained over a period of years to follow every order given to him by his commanders, had enough self-will to disobey Order 66, and allow a group of Jedi younglings to escape from the Empire. 147 was also very strong and muscular, as was shown by him possessing enough strength to tear a chain used to hold him—a full grown man—off of a wall. He also was able to defeat a heavily armored B2 super battle droid in close quarters combat by simply hitting it over the head with a hard piece of metal, albeit by surprise. 147 had [[brown]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], [[black]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]], tan skin, and, like all clone troopers, stood at 1.83 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall. ==Behind the scenes== HOB-147 made his first appearance in the [[Star Wars: Clone Wars Adventures (graphic novels)|''Clone Wars Adventures'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] ''[[Salvaged]]''. He later had a small entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''. ==Appearances== *{{CWACite|9|Salvaged}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |nl=HOB-147 }} [[Category:Clone stormtroopers]] [[Category:Clone trooper pilots]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]]
